real_architecture_summary: The executable path is promoted candidate rows -> compile_blueprint() -> DSL Blueprint JSONL -> ExecutableStrategySpec.from_blueprint() -> engine.run_engine_for_specs() -> DslInterpreterV1.generate_positions(). spec/strategies/*.yaml and project/compilers/spec_transformer.py are side surfaces rather than the active runtime contract.
top_invariants: Executable runtime contracts are ExecutableStrategySpec objects, not spec/strategies YAMLs.
Serialized blueprint lineage must survive the blueprint/spec/runtime boundary.
Compilation and clustering must be deterministic for the same inputs.

findings.title: Serialized blueprint lineage is silently reset when runtime rebuilds a blueprint
findings.evidence: build_blueprint() only maps a subset of LineageSpec fields, so fields such as proposal_id, wf_status, cost_config_digest, promotion_track, ontology_spec_hash, template_verb, and constraints revert to defaults during rebuild.
findings.why_it_matters: Artifact identity and provenance are lost exactly at the blueprint/spec -> runtime boundary.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Serialize a Blueprint with populated lineage fields, rebuild it with build_blueprint(), and compare lineage before/after.
findings.suggested_remediation: Round-trip the full LineageSpec payload or validate the entire lineage dict directly instead of cherry-picking fields.

findings.title: Runtime hard-requires funding_rate_scaled even for strategies that never reference funding
findings.evidence: build_signal_frame() raises if funding_rate_scaled is absent, and DslInterpreterV1.generate_positions() calls it before interpreting the blueprint, even for non-funding strategies.
findings.why_it_matters: Legal strategies can fail at runtime because the runtime contract is broader than the declared trigger/condition set.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Execute a non-funding blueprint with spread-only features and no funding columns.
findings.suggested_remediation: Make funding fields lazy or defaultable for strategies that do not use funding conditions.

findings.title: Post-compile clustering is nondeterministic because it uses Python hash()
findings.evidence: compile_strategy_blueprints.py computes cluster_key = hash((event_type, template_verb)) % 1000, and Python salts hash() per process.
findings.why_it_matters: The same inputs can keep or drop different blueprints across separate runs.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Run hash((event_type, template_verb)) % 1000 in separate interpreters and compare outputs.
findings.suggested_remediation: Use a stable digest or the explicit tuple as the clustering key.

findings.title: Main blueprint compiler bypasses its own selection helper and ignores several exposed gating arguments
findings.evidence: _choose_event_rows() exists and understands max_per_event and fallback/quality gates, but main() iterates every promoted row directly and never calls it.
findings.why_it_matters: Operator-facing CLI knobs appear to control compilation but do not change the output set.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Trace main() from argparse through the loop over edge_df.to_dict("records") and compare output counts across different --max_per_event values.
findings.suggested_remediation: Route main() through _choose_event_rows() or delete the dead CLI knobs.

findings.title: spec/strategies YAML files are not part of the executable pipeline and have drifted from runtime reality
findings.evidence: No executable path in scope loads spec/strategies/*.yaml; the engine consumes ExecutableStrategySpec objects built from DSL blueprints instead.
findings.why_it_matters: These YAML files look authoritative but do not govern runtime behavior.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Search the repo for spec/strategies references and compare with compile_strategy_blueprints.py and engine/runner.py.
findings.suggested_remediation: Either wire these YAMLs into an enforced compile path or demote them to examples/tests and point operators at Blueprint/ExecutableStrategySpec as the source of truth.

test_coverage_assessment: Tests cover DSL legality and some runtime contracts, but not lineage round-trip integrity, non-funding runtime execution without funding columns, clustering determinism, or the fact that main() ignores its own selection knobs.
docs_specs_code_contradictions: spec/strategies files look canonical, but the executable pipeline never loads them.
spec_transformer.py implies a StrategySpec path, while the engine actually consumes ExecutableStrategySpec from DSL blueprints.
suggested_regression_tests: A full Blueprint lineage round-trip test.
A non-funding runtime execution test without funding columns.
A multi-process determinism test for blueprint clustering.
open_questions_or_unknowns: Whether candidate_id is guaranteed unique enough for audit row keying inside compile_strategy_blueprints.py.
